task_completion.description: The agent opened and read the float sizing specification file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of /home/ga/Desktop/float_spec.txt.

task_completion.description: The agent added a new Fuselage or Pod component to serve as the float.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP Geometry Browser shows a newly added Fuselage or Pod component alongside the baseline model.

task_completion.description: The agent sized the new float component to the specified length and diameter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Design/Geometry window in OpenVSP shows the length parameter set to approximately 5.8 and the diameter/cross-section parameters adjusted to approximately 0.7.

task_completion.description: The agent positioned the float below and to the side of the fuselage using XForm controls.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The XForm tab in OpenVSP shows the X Location set to ~0.8, Y Location to ~1.4, and Z Location to ~-1.8. The 3D viewport reflects this offset position.

task_completion.description: The agent enabled XZ-Plane symmetry or manually mirrored the float to create a twin float configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sym tab in OpenVSP shows XZ symmetry enabled for the float component, and the 3D viewport clearly displays two identical floats on either side of the aircraft.

task_completion.description: The agent saved the modified aircraft model to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An OpenVSP 'Save Vehicle As' dialog or terminal command showing the file being saved exactly as /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/floatplane_variant.vsp3.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenVSP graphical interface to modify the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenVSP application windows (Geometry browser, XForm tab, Sym tab), rather than just text-based editing of the .vsp3 XML file.

integrity.description: The agent actually modified the 3D model rather than faking the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport in OpenVSP dynamically updates to show the floats being added, sized, and positioned relative to the baseline Cessna 210 model.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file using OpenVSP's save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP 'Save Vehicle As' dialog is visible, or the main application window title updates to reflect the new filename 'floatplane_variant.vsp3'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target float length is 5.8m (acceptable range: 5.2m to 6.4m). The target position is X=0.8m, Y=1.4m, Z=-1.8m (acceptable range for each coordinate is ±0.3m). The expected output file must be saved exactly at /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/floatplane_variant.vsp3.
